steps.title: Compute the third side using perimeter
steps.content:
The perimeter is 32 m and two sides are 8 m and 9 m. Let the third side be $x$. Then
$$x = 32 - 8 - 9 = 15$$
So the third side must be 15 meters.

steps.title: Verify triangle inequality and obtuse condition
steps.content:
Check triangle inequality:
$$8 + 9 > 15$$ (true)
$$8 + 15 > 9$$ (true)
$$9 + 15 > 8$$ (true)
All three hold, so a triangle exists.
To be obtuse, the square of the longest side must exceed the sum of the squares of the other two sides. The longest side is 15 m:
$$15^2 = 225$$
$$8^2 + 9^2 = 64 + 81 = 145$$
Since $$225 > 145$$, the triangle is obtuse.
Therefore the length 15 meters satisfies all conditions. That matches choice D — D is correct.
